content.problemStatement:
You are on a one-dimensional number line, initially at x=1x = 1x=1. You want to reach x=N+1x = N + 1x=N+1.

You can do the following 222 actions when you are at x(x‚â§N)x (x \le N)x(x‚â§N):


Pay a cost of AxA_xAx‚Äã and move to x+1x + 1x+1.
Pay a cost of BxB_xBx‚Äã and move to a random position among the positions x=1,2,‚Ä¶,N+1x = 1, 2, \ldots, N + 1x=1,2,‚Ä¶,N+1. All of the N+1N + 1N+1 possibilities are equiprobable.
Find the minimum expected cost to reach N+1N + 1N+1 if we make decisions optimally.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
The third line contains NNN integers - B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum expected cost to reach N+1N + 1N+1.


Your answer will be marked correct if either the absolute or the relative error does not exceed 10‚àí610^{-6}10‚àí6.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The optimal strategy is to always use the randomize operation when we are at x=1x = 1x=1. Then, the probability that we reach x=2x = 2x=2 in exactly iii turns can be shown to be 12i\dfrac{1}{2^i}2i1‚Äã and the associated cost is 2‚ãÖi2 \cdot i2‚ãÖi.
Summing up the infinite series, ‚àëi=1inf‚Å°2‚ãÖi‚ãÖ12i\sum_{i = 1}^{\inf} 2 \cdot i \cdot \dfrac{1}{2^i}‚àëi=1inf‚Äã2‚ãÖi‚ãÖ2i1‚Äã, we get 444.
Test Case 2 : The optimal strategy is just to use the normal operation of going to x+1x + 1x+1 when we are at x=1x = 1x=1. The guaranteed cost of this operation is 222.
